name: Jason Wasgatt
note: d. 2 January 1866, æ 66 yrs., 5 mos. Schoolteacher of Cromwell's Harbor; "strong, earnest abolitionist."

name: Abby (Rodick) Wasgatt
note: d. 14 April 1882, æ 82. Outlived her husband by sixteen years and her eldest son by nineteen

name: B[enjamin] R. Wasgatt
note: no dates. Cenotaph. Buried in fact at Chalmette National Cemetery, Louisiana, grave 2298, d. 10 May 1863 of "debility" at the New Orleans Ma

name: Elmer E[llsworth] Wasgatt
note: d. 30 September 1877, æ 15 yrs., 7 mos., 15 days (b. 15 February 1862). Named for the first Union officer killed in the Civil War

name: Infant daughter of Mr. and Mrs. W. H
note: b. 28 December 188[?]; d. 29 December [same year]. Top of stone broken; year unreadable

name: Capt. David Rodick
note: husband of Sally Stanwood; the patriarch's son and Fountain's grandfather

name: Sally Stanwood Rodick
note: wife of Capt. David

name: Daniel Rodick
note: farmer and fisherman of Cromwell's Harbor; opened the cottages that became the Rodick House

name: Deborah Wasgatt Stanwood Rodick
note: wife of Daniel

name: John Rodick
note: d. 11 June 1854, æ 68 yrs

name: Benjamin Rodick
note: son of Daniel; member of Co. C, 1st Maine Heavy Artillery

name: Thomas S. Rodick
note: d. 22 July 1876, æ 51 yrs

name: Capt. David Rodick, Jr
note: husband of Marian L.; father of Fountain, Serenus, Flora, Milton, Edward

name: Marian L. Rodick
note: first wife of Capt. David, Jr

name: Milton Rodick
note: infant son of David Jr. and Marian; died three months before his mother

name: Fountain Rodick
note: son of David Jr.; treasurer of the Bar Harbor Water Company; last living son of the popular proprietor of the Rodick House

name: Emily Rodick
note: daughter of David and Betsey

name: Kate T. Rodick
note: wife of Walter E

name: David O. Rodick
note: a generation later; husband of Florence E

name: Flora Rodick Pineo
note: daughter of David Jr.; sister of Fountain and Serenus; wife of Judge Charles B. Pineo; buried under her married name in the PINEO lot

name: Stephen H Beverly
note: Eldest son of James and Eunice Beverly. Died at twenty-three; no cause listed in the records.

name: William D Beverly
note: Stephen's younger brother. Enlisted in the Union Army on October 25, 1861 in Catlettsburg, Kentucky, with the Thirteenth Infantry. Died in Bar Harbor a few months later, at twenty-six.

name: Betsey Brewer
note: Born Betsey Higgins on June 24, 1836 — daughter of Stephen and Ann Maria Higgins. Married Edward L. Brewer in March 1860. Their only son Edgar was born the following year. She died as a young mother in September 1864 at twenty-eight.

name: Edward L. Brewer Jr.
note: A Hulls Cove farmer; his father (also Edward) was a shipbuilder who ran sawmills on Duck Brook and Hulls Cove Brook. Enlisted in the Civil War at twenty-two. After Betsey died he married her cousin Zena W. Higgins. He died at eighty-one in 1915.

name: Zena Wasgatt (Higgins) Brewer
note: Daughter of Captain Zaccheus Higgins and Sarah Leland, born November 27, 1822. She first married S. Blithen Higgins in 1844 and was widowed at thirty-two when he died in 1856 in ill health. She had been engaged to Capt. John Leland, but his ship wrecked in a gale off Cape Cod in 1863 and he was lost at sea. She later married Edward L. Brewer. She died of bronchitis in September 1896 at seventy-four.

name: Mary E (Leland) Bunker
note: Wife of Eri L. Bunker. Died at twenty-three in December 1880.

name: Abbie E. Cole
note: Buried at MDS Cemetery — d. 22 Jan 1884

name: Julia J. Conners
note: Wife of John Conners. Died at eighty-two in March 1894, six years after her husband.

name: Thomas R. Salisbury
note: Son of Reuben Salisbury and Almira M. Salisbury. Died very young in January 1836.

name: Capt Bethuel Salsbury
note: Sea captain. Died at sixty-five in July 1872. Note the different spelling — Salsbury vs. Salisbury — used by this branch of the family.

name: Erma Victoria (Stafford) Shaw
note: Wife of Norman Shaw. Lived 1905 to 1982.

name: Mary Ellen (Livinghouse) Shaw
note: Modern Shaw descendant. Lived 1937 to 2017.

name: Judge Norman Shaw
note: Husband of Erma V. (Stafford) Shaw. Lived 1892 to 1973.

name: Norman Pineo Shaw
note: Modern Shaw descendant. Lived 1928 to 2023.

name: Edwin F Smith
note: Son of Edwin F. and Florinda Smith. Died at seven on December 13, 1874 — a week before his mother.

name: Florinda Smith
note: Wife of Edwin F. Smith. Died at thirty on December 20, 1874, a week after her son.

name: Laura E. Soper
note: Daughter of George E. and Nellie D. Soper. Died in her sixties on a date partly illegible on the stone.

name: Ralph E. Soper
note: Son of George E. and Nellie D. Soper. Died at eight in December 1891.

name: Hannah (Higgins) Stanwood
note: Wife of Humphrey Stanwood, born a Higgins (1777). Died at seventy-three in April 1851.

name: Humphrey Bradstreet Stanwood
note: Husband of Hannah Higgins Stanwood. Lived to eighty, dying in October 1847.

name: Isaac Chase Staples
note: Husband of Martha A. Richards Staples. Died in the Civil War.

name: Martha Amelia (Richards) Staples
note: Wife of Isaac Chase Staples. Died at twenty-four in August 1862.

name: Angelia J. (McFarland) Stover
note: A McFarland by birth, married into the Stover line. Lived 1849 to 1910.

name: Crawford A. Stover
note: Husband of Mary Ann (Dawes) Stover. Lived from March 1828 to August 1911.

name: Mary Ann (Dawes) Stover
note: Wife of Crawford A. Stover.

name: Sarah Suminsbey
note: First wife of Jacob S. Suminsby. Died at twenty-one in March 1852.

name: Deborah Suminsby
note: Died at eighteen in March of the 1810s. Relation to Jacob and Sarah unclear.

name: Experience L. Suminsby
note: Second wife of Jacob S. Suminsby. Died at seventy-three in January 1892, six months before her husband.

name: Jacob S. Suminsby
note: Husband of Sarah and (later) Experience. Died on July 4, 1892 at seventy-five.

name: Cora A. (Dorr) Tabbut
note: Eldest daughter of Thomas and Olive Dorr; born in Columbia Falls in April 1861. Married Milton Hilman Tabbutt on Christmas Day, 1880, in her hometown. Had three children. Died at twenty-five in June 1886, a year after her mother and two of her sisters.

name: Ephraim H Walls
note: Husband of Clara A. Walls. Died at thirty in January 1861.

name: Vesta Ella Walls
note: Daughter of Ephraim H. and Clara A. Walls. Born during her father's final summer; died at three in February 1863.

name: Elmer E. Wasgall
note: Also recorded as Elmer Ellsworth Wasgatt

name: Corp B R Wasgalt
note: Also recorded as B[enjamin] R. Wasgatt

name: Abigail “Abby” (Rodick) Wasgatt
note: Also recorded as Abby (Rodick) Wasgatt

name: John Woodberry
note: Died at eighty-one in October 1875.

name: Charlotte S. (Gilley) Young
note: Wife of Willard W. Young. Died at twenty-three in January 1863.

name: Etta Maria Young
note: Daughter of Elisha and Priscilla Young. Died at two in February 1865.

name: Pvt Ezra H. Young
note: Son of Joseph and Hannah Young. Died on January 13, 1863, in Washington, D.C., as a soldier in the 26th Maine Regiment. He was twenty-one.

name: George Dallas Young
note: Son of Joseph and Hannah Young. Died at twenty-seven in November 1872.

name: Priscilla Woodward (Kelley) Young
note: Wife of Elisha Young; mother of Etta Maria and Winnie J. Died at forty-six in December 1886.

name: Royal Young
note: Husband of Sarah B. Manchester. Lived 1843 to 1913.

name: Winnie J. Young
note: Daughter of Elisha and Priscilla Young. Died at six in August 1873.

name: Zella B. Cook
note: Buried at MDS Cemetery — 12 May 1864 – 22 Jan 1885

name: Laura A. Cunningham
note: Buried at MDS Cemetery — d. 18 May 1886

name: John W Day
note: Son of Moses and Mary B. Day. There may be a Civil War letter connection — the family appears to overlap with a different John W. Day who served in the war.

name: Ethel Z. Dorr
note: Daughter of Thomas T. and Olive E. Dorr. Died of diphtheria at six years old on January 24, 1885 — a week after her older sister Lura.

name: Lura A. Dorr
note: Daughter of Thomas T. and Olive E. Dorr. Died of diphtheria at sixteen on January 17, 1885.

name: Olive E (Lord) Dorr
note: Wife of Thomas T. Dorr, mother of Lura, Ethel, and Cora. Died of diphtheria in Bar Harbor on March 14, 1885 at forty-eight — two months after she lost both of her younger daughters.

name: Lucreatia Kenny Douglass
note: Also recorded as Lucretia Kenny Douglass

name: Frances C Eveleth
note: Buried at MDS Cemetery — d. 19 Apr 1883

name: Gracie Frost
note: Daughter of George E. and L. J. Frost. Died at five in January 1885 during the same diphtheria winter as the Dorr children.

name: Antoinette A. “Abby” Grace
note: Daughter of Moses and Hannah L. Grace. Died at four in November 1858.

name: Hannah L. (Higgins) Grace
note: Wife of Moses Grace, born a Higgins. Died at forty-eight in January 1863.

name: Moses Grace
note: Husband of Hannah L. Higgins Grace. Lived to eighty-two, dying in October 1888.

name: Georgia E Grindle
note: Born Georgia Mills, also recorded as Georgie or Georgetta. Her first husband was Arthur W. Richardson, a dry goods peddler; they had two sons together, Murray and Elmer. After her divorce she married George D. Grindle in September 1889 and gave birth to their daughter Etta five months later — likely the reason for the divorce and remarriage. She died of unspecified causes in November 1891.

name: Clara B. (Rodick) Hamor
note: Born Clarissa Rodick; married James Hamor in 1822. Outlived her husband by fifteen years, dying in June 1888 at eighty-four.

name: Capt James Hamor
note: Born in Bar Harbor in 1794, son of David and Experience Thompson Hamor. Donated the land where the Bar Harbor Congregational Church now stands to be used as a school. Served as village postmaster in the 1850s and 1860s. Died at seventy-nine in December 1873.

name: Christopher Havens
note: Husband of Mary Higgins Havens. Lived to eighty-three, dying in April 1881.

name: Israel Havens
note: Son of Christopher and Mary. Died at twenty-two in January 1849.

name: Julia E. Havens
note: Daughter of Christopher and Mary. Died at eighteen in June 1863.

name: Mary (Higgins) Havens
note: Wife of Christopher Havens, born a Higgins (1791). Lived to eighty-five, dying in October 1877.

name: Mercy Havens
note: Daughter of Christopher and Mary, possibly born around 1824. Died in June 1875.

name: Sally S. Havens
note: Daughter of Christopher and Mary. Died young in February 1840.

name: Addie Higgins
note: Daughter of Charles and Almira H. Higgins. Died at eighteen months in October 1865.

name: Addie Florence Higgins
note: Daughter of Leander A. and Mary Higgins. Died at six months in May 1871.

name: Albert F Higgins
note: Died at seventy-five in March 1898.

name: Albertena Higgins
note: Daughter of George P. and Eudora Higgins. Died at eleven months in January 1849.

name: Angelia S. Higgins
note: Wife of Gilbert F. Higgins. Died at nineteen in January 1849.

name: Annette C (Allen) Higgins
note: Lived 1848 to 1915.

name: Asa Higgins
note: Husband of Mercy S. Higgins. Died at seventy-three in September 1877.

name: Daniel Rodick Higgins
note: Husband of Hannah Higgins. Lived to seventy-three, dying in November 1889.

name: Eddie M. Higgins
note: Son of Leander A. and Mary Higgins. Died at four in August 1873.

name: Emily L. Higgins
note: Daughter of Cyrenius and Emily Higgins. Died at twenty-one in September 1862.

name: Etta J. Higgins
note: Daughter of William H. and Mary Higgins. Died at five in February 1864.

name: Florence A. Higgins
note: Wife of Edwin H. Higgins. Died at thirty-one in August 1873.

name: George Purkett Higgins
note: Husband of Eudora Higgins. Died at twenty-two in April 1849.

name: Hannah (Higgins) Higgins
note: Wife of Daniel Higgins. Lived to eighty-three, dying in January 1901.

name: Humphrey S. Higgins
note: Listed as a member of Co. H, 4th Maine Regiment, on his gravestone.

name: Capt Isreal Higgins
note: Also recorded as Capt. Israel Higgins, Jr.

name: Laura Higgins
note: Died at fourteen in December 1859.

name: Leander A. Higgins
note: Husband of Mary Higgins, father of Eddie M. and Addie Florence. Died at thirty-four in September 1873.

name: Mamie Higgins
note: Sister of Andy Higgins. Dates unknown.

name: Mercy C (Stanwood) Higgins
note: Wife of Oliver Higgins 3rd, born a Stanwood (1803). Died at eighty-three in February 1887.

name: Mercy S. Higgins
note: Wife of Asa Higgins. Died at sixty-five in December 1862.

name: PVT Simon Higgins
note: Also recorded as Simon Higgins

name: Adaline (Higgins) Hotchkiss
note: Wife of John A. Hotchkiss. Died at thirty-four in February 1845.

name: Ruby (Pineo) Jackson
note: Born a Pineo in 1858; lived until 1944. Buried in the Pineo family plot.

name: Sean Philip Kane
note: Buried at MDS Cemetery — d. 27 Oct 1979

name: Virginia Louise (Shaw) Kane
note: Buried at MDS Cemetery — 7 May 1930 – 6 Oct 2002

name: Adaline H. (Higgins) Leland
note: Wife of Captain Oren H. Leland. Mother of Flora A. Higgins (later Grindle). Died at twenty-six in February 1858 — no cause listed on her death certificate.

name: Ansel B Leland
note: Son of Enoch H. and Irene Leland. Died at five years and ten months in May 1852.

name: Ellen S. Leland
note: Daughter of Enoch H. and Irene Leland. Died in her thirties in August 1882.

name: Enoch Higgins Leland
note: Husband of Irene Leland, father of Ansel and Ellen. Lived to fifty-six, dying in February 1877.

name: Mrs Irene (Rodick) Leland
note: Wife of Enoch H. Leland. Outlived her husband by twenty-five years, dying in March 1902 at eighty.

name: Daniel Lynch
note: Died at seventy-four in January 1874. Nothing further known.

name: Sarah B. Manchester
note: Wife of Royal Young. Lived 1833 to 1914.

name: Margaret (Kane) McFarland
note: Wife of Rodney McFarland.

name: Rodney McFarland
note: Husband of Margaret Kane McFarland. Lived 1824 to 1905.

name: Andrew Watson Moore
note: Died at sixty-five in April 1906.

name: Harland C. “Harley” Parsons
note: Son of L. E. and Clara H. Parsons. Died at three and a half in October 1884.

name: Mary Ellen Pineo
note: Buried in the Pineo family plot.

name: Norman Pineo
note: Buried in the Pineo family plot.

name: Ella A. Pray
note: Daughter of E. H. and Nellie F. Pray. Died at seventeen months in October 1882.

name: Cassie May Richards
note: Daughter of unknown Richards parents. Died at eight months on a date partly illegible on the stone — sometime in February of the 1880s.

name: Henrietta G Richards
note: Wife of James H. Richards. Died at thirty-seven in June 1880.

name: Josiah B. Richards
note: Died at sixty-two on an illegible date.

name: Sarah Rebecca Richardson
note: Buried with Amos and Bethiah Richardson, the parents of Amanda M. (Richardson) Ash. Died in December 1908 at seventy-two.

name: Barbara D. (Davis) Roberts
note: Also recorded as Barbara D. Roberts

name: Cora Livingston (Frost) Roberts
note: Born a Frost in 1896.

name: George Roberts
note: Husband of Hannah Roberts. Died at sixty-two in October 1862. No known relation to the Tobias Roberts hotel family covered in the main Roberts bio.

name: Hannah (Haynes) Roberts
note: Wife of George Roberts. Died at thirty-seven in April 1861.

name: Mariam H (Ash) Roberts
note: Also recorded as Mariam H. Ash Roberts

name: CDR William M. Roberts
note: Also recorded as William M. Roberts

name: Almira M. Salisbury
note: Wife of Reuben Salisbury. Died at fifty-nine in February 1866.

name: Ellen R Salisbury
note: Wife of Warren H. Salisbury. Died at twenty in January 1856.

name: Olevia A. Salisbury
note: Daughter of Captain Bethuel and Elizabeth Salsbury. Died at nineteen in December 1865.

note: d. Birth and death dates unknown.

note: d. Birth and death dates unknown.

note: d. 16 Mar

name: Tobias Lord Roberts III
note: Grandson of Tobias Sr., son of Tobias L. Jr.; 8 January 1888 – 19 November 1956
